cards.back: A casting operator that safely converts pointers/references along an inheritance hierarchy, performing runtime type checking

cards.front: What does dynamic_cast return if the cast fails with a pointer?
cards.back: It returns nullptr if the cast is invalid (the object is not actually of the target type).

cards.front: What does dynamic_cast do if the cast fails with a reference?
cards.back: It throws a std::bad_cast exception (since references can't be null).

cards.question: What does this code do?
cards.answer: Safely attempts to downcast Base* to Derived*. If successful, calls derivedFunction(). If not, prints error message.
cards.explanation: dynamic_cast checks at runtime if basePtr actually points to a Derived object. If yes, returns valid pointer. If no, returns nullptr.

cards.text: dynamic_cast only works with {{c1::polymorphic}} classes (classes with at least one {{c2::virtual}} function).

cards.back: Casting from a base class pointer/reference to a derived class pointer/reference

cards.front: Why is dynamic_cast safer than static_cast for downcasting?
cards.back: dynamic_cast performs runtime type checking and returns nullptr/throws exception if invalid. static_cast does no checking and can create invalid pointers, causing undefined behavior.

cards.question: What's wrong with this code?
cards.answer: Compilation error: dynamic_cast requires Base to be polymorphic (have at least one virtual function).
cards.explanation: dynamic_cast only works with polymorphic types. Add a virtual destructor to Base to make it polymorphic.

Use dynamic_cast when:
  • You need to safely downcast from base to derived
  • You're not sure of the actual object type at runtime
  • You want runtime type checking and safe failure
Avoid if:
  • You can redesign to use virtual functions instead
  • Performance is critical (dynamic_cast has runtime cost)
  • The type is known at compile time
examples: // When base pointer might be different derived types:

cards.front: What is the performance cost of dynamic_cast?
cards.back: dynamic_cast has runtime overhead because it must traverse the inheritance hierarchy to verify the cast is valid. It's slower than static_cast or C-style casts.

cards.explanation:
Dynamic casting often indicates design issues. Better alternatives:
  • Use virtual functions for behavior instead of type checking
  • Use visitor pattern for type-specific operations
  • Store type info if you frequently need it
  • Redesign to avoid downcasting
Excessive dynamic_cast usage suggests the design doesn't fully leverage polymorphism.
